commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
The stacked branch extends the doctor structured repair contract across shell completion, systemd/startup/browser maintenance, legacy plugin manifests, configured-plugin installs, and plugin registry state with health-check effects, notes, dry-run/diff output, and focused tests.

Reproducibility: yes. source-reproducible on the PR head. The disabled registry issue is produced by `detectPluginRegistryStateIssues`, while the repair path returns no status and the runner treats missing status as repaired.
commands.review_followup: A narrow automated repair can address the disabled-path accounting defect; stack order and broader upgrade proof remain maintainer decisions.

target.title: Doctor: continue structured repair conversion
target.body:
## Goal

Convert the plugin/config repair bucket to the structured doctor contract so package/config/state repairs are detectable, previewable, and validated through the same path.

This PR exists to make plugin-registry and configured-plugin install maintenance auditable from first principles:

- detect must be read-only and silent
- repair must report concrete config/package/state effects
- dry-run must describe real work without fabricating diffs
- non-dry-run repair must be followed by scoped detect-after validation

## Scope

| Check | Goal | Behavior |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `core/doctor/legacy-plugin-manifests` | Move legacy manifest capability metadata into the current contract shape. | Detect finds legacy top-level capability keys; repair rewrites only detected manifests. |
| `core/doctor/configured-plugin-installs` | Ensure configured plugin/channel installs are accounted for after release updates. | Detect reports stale release repair metadata; repair installs configured plugin/channel packages and stamps release metadata. |
| `core/doctor/plugin-registry` | Make registry/package repair a structured state repair instead of a print-oriented helper. | Detect reports stale registry/package/peer-link issues; repair refreshes registry/package state without terminal printing. |

## Structured Repair Matrix

| Check | Detect / lint behavior | Repair behavior | Dry-run / diff / effects | Detect-after proof |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `core/doctor/legacy-plugin-manifests` | Reads manifests and reports legacy top-level capability keys as findings. | Rewrites only detected manifest migrations under `contracts`. | Concrete file diff plus file effect: `would-rewrite-legacy-plugin-manifest-contracts` / `rewrite-legacy-plugin-manifest-contracts`. | Scoped validation confirms legacy top-level capability keys are gone after rewrite. |
| `core/doctor/configured-plugin-installs` | Reports stale release repair metadata when configured plugin installs need the release repair marker. | Installs configured plugin/channel packages as before and stamps release metadata. | Config diff for `meta`; package effects for plugin/channel installs; config effect for `meta.lastTouchedVersion`. | Scoped validation confirms current release metadata no longer reports the configured install finding. |
| `core/doctor/plugin-registry` | `detectPluginRegistryStateIssues(...)` is read-only and silent. | Converted repair calls `repairPluginRegistryState(...)` directly; legacy `maybeRepairPluginRegistryState(...)` remains the print wrapper for old non-fix paths. | State/package effects for registry refresh, stale managed package removal, and peer-link repair; no synthetic diff for package-manager work. | Registry/package tests assert package directory, lockfile, registry record, peer-link state, and clean detect-after. |
| Note-phase contract | No terminal output from structured detect. | Structured repair returns `{ config, changes, warnings }` and does not call `note(...)`; no bespoke note sink is used. | Presentation stays owned by the base runner/contribution layer. | Audit proof on current head confirms the bucket follows the finalized note contract. |

## Proof

| Proof | Result |
| --- | --- |
| Focused plugin registry/core/repair/lint tests | 4 focused files / 37 tests passed on current head. |
| Earlier Crabbox proof | Build, focused tests, release configured-plugin tests, tsgo, and real doctor CLI lint proof passed in run `run_40f3018e5b28`. |
| Formatting and whitespace checks | Passed. |
| Typecheck proof | Full repo `tsgo --noEmit` passed in the recorded proof. |

## Out of Scope

- Adding new public CLI flags.
- Changing plugin install policy.
- Treating package-manager work as if it had a file diff when no stable before/after file exists.

## Real Behavior Proof

Behavior addressed: Structured doctor repair conversion for configured plugin install and plugin registry flows; lint no longer reports configured installs once the release repair marker is current; real CLI lint reports the intended structured checks.

Real environment tested: Direct AWS Crabbox lease `cbx_95f6e9c97891` (`silver-crab`), OpenClaw source checkout synced from this branch, Node v24.15.0, pnpm 11.1.0.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: build, focused doctor tests, release configured-plugin tests, tsgo, and real `openclaw doctor --lint --only ... --json` checks against synthetic old/current config metadata and empty registry state.

Evidence after fix: Crabbox run `run_40f3018e5b28` exited 0. Proof images are posted in https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/84290#issuecomment-4491556493 and stored in https://gist.github.com/giodl73-repo/e41418e607535f7d29451ff7329e8e00.

Observed result after fix: old configured install config reports `core/doctor/configured-plugin-installs`; current release marker returns clean; empty registry reports `core/doctor/plugin-registry`; focused tests and typecheck passed.

What was not tested: full changed gate in direct Crabbox because the raw Crabbox sync path excludes `.git`; package, Docker, and update-survivor lanes were not run for this narrow structured repair slice.
target.author: giodl73-repo
